reasoning: Q6 at 35.2ms is 1.76x slower than DuckDB (20ms) but already sub-50ms and faster than PostgreSQL. Zone maps are defined but unused, representing 50-70% potential I/O reduction. With 2 iterations remaining and clear, high-confidence optimizations available, aggressive execution can close the ~15ms gap by addressing the dominant scan_filter bottleneck (53.7% of time).
focus_areas: Implement zone map block pruning to skip 67% of blocks on date predicate (biggest I/O win)
Add AVX-512 vectorized filtering for compound predicate evaluation (CPU acceleration)
Optimize HDD madvise hints to support zone map targeted reads (I/O efficiency)
strategy_notes: Prioritize zone map implementation first (recommendation 0) - this is the critical missing piece causing 50-70% unnecessary I/O. Once zone maps skip irrelevant blocks, secondary optimizations (SIMD filtering, predicate reordering) will show their full impact. The data is sorted by l_shipdate, making zone map pruning especially effective for the date range predicate. Expected combined improvement: 50-70% on scan_filter time (18.9ms → 6-9ms) plus 20-30% on filter CPU (4-6ms saved) = total scan_filter ~4-6ms, bringing overall query to 20-25ms range. This closes the DuckDB gap to 1.0-1.25x, achieving competitive parity.
notes: Recommendation 3 (HDD-aware madvise) included because zone map pruning changes the access pattern from sequential reads to targeted block access, making madvise recalibration important. Recommendations 2 and 4-6 deferred to iteration 2 if needed. Avoid Kahan summation simplification (rec 6, medium risk) - keep numeric accuracy since aggregation is not the bottleneck.
